response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack software developer with web, mobile, and some embedded experience, but the role requires deep hardware/SoC expertise — AI accelerator architecture, SIMD/MIMD, GPU workload analysis, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in the resume. The brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments and a wave-field-synthesis thesis are the closest adjacent experience, and they are far from sufficient for a Principal Performance Architect position.
strengths: Long, varied senior engineering career across multiple languages and stacks
Some low-level/C++ exposure (National Instruments LabWindows/CVI, bachelor thesis on wave field synthesis)
Demonstrated ability to optimize high-throughput systems (Sociomantic low-latency DLang services)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, AI-accelerator, or hardware-software co-design experience
No deep-learning workload optimization, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD background
Educational/experience profile is web/full-stack focused, not a relevant engineering or CS research path for a Principal-level hardware architect role

key_factors.factor: Lacks SoC/IP modeling and AI accelerator hardware experience
No DNN training/inference optimization or GPU architecture work
Senior-level engineering tenure and some low-level C/C++ background
